title: 10 best herbal capsules for gastritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with gastritis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal the stomach lining. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, licorice capsules can help to protect the stomach mucosa from damage caused by stomach acid, promoting a healthy digestive environment and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach lining associated with gastritis.
Licoric Acid: As a natural antacid, licoric acid can help neutralize stomach acid and relieve symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and indigestion caused by gastritis.
Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have protective effects on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach lining, alleviating gastritis symptoms.

Flavonoids (particularly quercetin): As antioxidants, flavonoids help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing gastritis inflammation.
Licoricidin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, alleviating gastritis symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, making them effective in treating gastritis.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the plant contains glycosides and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the stomach lining and reduce gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier and sift out impurities.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of 2g of the powder and 1g of the gelatin mixture for 30 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with gastritis because they contain soothing compounds that calm inflammation and reduce irritation in the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile work to ease symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and cramps associated with gastritis. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and discomfort. As a result, chamomile capsules provide effective relief from gastritis-related discomfort, promoting a sense of calm and digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and swelling in the stomach lining, which helps to alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as nausea and abdominal pain.
Antacid properties: The soothing and calming effects of chamomile help to neutralize stomach acid and relieve heartburn and indigestion associated with gastritis.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with gastritis by reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.

Matricaria chamomilla essential oil (containing bisabolol): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm the stomach and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis, providing relief from symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part for treating gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for gastritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Seeds: They are sometimes used to make capsules for gastritis due to their potential to help soothe and calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a clean and dry environment.
Dry the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers in a low temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 2 grams of cellulose and 1 gram of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a powder funnel and capsule filler for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The active compound gingerol in ginger capsules has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling in the stomach lining. Additionally, ginger's natural carminative properties help to expel gas and ease bloating, providing relief from gastritis symptoms such as abdominal discomfort and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Herbal ginger capsules contain enzymes that stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to break down food more efficiently and reducing discomfort associated with gastritis.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's high antioxidant content helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gastritis symptoms like heartburn, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with gastritis, such as pain and swelling in the stomach lining.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also sesquiterpene compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in gastritis patients.

Zingibain: This proteolytic enzyme has anti-inflammatory and anti-ulcer properties, which help break down excess mucus and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Zingiber officinale Roots are also widely used, as they contain essential oils and other bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining in gastritis patients.

Stems: Although less commonly used than Rhizomes and Roots, Zingiber officinale Stems are sometimes utilized due to their ability to absorb and store nutrients, which can contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a sharp knife or garden tool in late summer.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently brushing off dirt with a soft brush for 10 minutes.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10-20 grams of cellulose or vegetable cellulose in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 0-00 capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves by cutting them at the base of the plant with scissors or a knife.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 100 milligrams of the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 50 milligrams of filler material like starch or cellulose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, which effectively soothes and calms the stomach lining. This natural remedy reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain commonly associated with gastritis. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage, promoting a healthy gut environment that facilitates proper digestion and absorption of nutrients.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric capsules' curcumin content helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a primary cause of gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant Activity: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage stomach tissue and contribute to gastritis, helping to promote healing and protect against further irritation.

Gastroprotective Compounds: Certain compounds in turmeric, such as curcuminoids, have been shown to directly protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, ulcers, and other irritants, which can exacerbate gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.

Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from gastritis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another curcuminoid, has shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also used for their curcumin content, which is believed to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.
Buds: Buds of the Curcuma longa plant are sometimes used due to their high antioxidant content, which may help protect the stomach from damage and promote recovery from gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 250ml of water and 10g of gelatin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly for 10 minutes or until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine and 500mg per capsule.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, aloe leaf extracts are often used to soothe and calm gastrointestinal issues associated with gastritis.

Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of bioactive compounds, rhyzome extracts have been traditionally used to treat digestive disorders such as gastritis, promoting gut health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from fresh leaves and filter it through cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Mix the filtered aloe vera gel with 200mg of dried ginger powder and 100mg of peppermint leaf extract.
Add 500ml of distilled water to the mixture and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Allow the mixture to cool and strain it through a coffee filter to remove any sediment or impurities.
Fill empty capsules with the cooled mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram capsules helps with gastritis because they contain the herb Origanum majorana, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, wild marjoram's antimicrobial properties may help to eliminate Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. By reducing inflammation and eliminating bacterial infections, herbal wild marjoram capsules can provide relief from gastritis symptoms and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wild marjoram contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, reducing pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Gastroprotective properties: Wild marjoram's bioactive compounds have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid, bile, and other irritants, thereby preventing further inflammation and healing existing lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild marjoram plant are used to make medicinal capsules for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues.

Flowers: Wild marjoram flowers are used in medicinal capsules for gastritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation, relax spasms, and provide relief from symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oregano leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried origanum vulgare flowers.
Clean and dry the harvested oregano to remove any dirt or moisture using a clean cloth.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried origanum vulgare flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400-500 mg of the ground oregano powder with 200-300 mg of vegetable cellulose capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the oregano-capsule mixture, yielding approximately 50-60 capsules per batch.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen capsules helps with gastritis because they contain methyl salicylate, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, wintergreen's antimicrobial properties may help to eliminate Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, further contributing to its beneficial effects on digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen's primary compound, methyl salicylate, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis, allowing for faster healing and relief from symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in wintergreen capsules help neutralize free radicals and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate gastritis and other digestive issues.

Pain-relieving properties: Wintergreen's salicylic acid content provides natural pain relief for individuals suffering from gastritis-related discomfort, such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain, allowing for improved quality of life.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gastritis.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues like gastritis due to their ability to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems.
Crush the collected plant material into small pieces weighing 200g.
Mix the crushed material with 400ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the essence.
Fill gelatin capsules with the extracted essence and seal immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and luteolin, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis such as pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of purple coneflower may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis and its associated symptoms.
Gastric mucosa protection: Purple coneflower's saponin content has been shown to strengthen the gastric mucosa, reducing the risk of ulcers and other damage that can exacerbate gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastritis, due to their ability to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop 20-30 grams of the harvested flowers into small pieces.
Combine chopped flowers with 500ml of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl and discard solids.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with 250mg of the cooled liquid extract and seal immediately.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with gastritis because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds called polyphenols, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties that combat Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. Additionally, purple coneflower contains antioxidants that soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gastritis. As a result, herbal purple coneflower capsules can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gastritis are a natural remedy designed to alleviate the symptoms of gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the stomach lining, leading to discomfort, pain, and digestive issues. The best herbal capsules for gastritis include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Mentha x piperita, Foeniculum vulgare, Astragalus membranaceus, Panax ginseng, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, gastritis can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, weight loss, and social isolation, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastritis that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues such as ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal capsules containing extracts from plants like turmeric, ginger, and slippery elm may help alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and inflammation. Other conditions, including acid reflux, stomach cramps, and diarrhea, can also be managed with herbal supplements, providing a natural alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with gastritis is a blend of slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root. Slippery elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes, while licorice root reduces inflammation and calms digestive issues. Marshmallow root adds its anti-inflammatory properties and helps to heal the stomach lining. A balanced ratio of these herbs can effectively alleviate gastritis symptoms, promoting a healthy digestive system and reducing discomfort. This combination is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat gastritis and related digestive issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, herbal capsules can be a good option for managing gastritis due to their high efficacy and safety profile. The study, which involved 1137 patients, found that phytopreparations, including herbal capsules, were well tolerated with no major side effects and were effective in treating various gastrointestinal disorders, including mild and moderate gastritis. Specifically, the study noted that preparations containing artichoke, silymarin, and Iberogast were frequently used and effective in treating gastrointestinal complaints.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with gastritis because it contains licorice root, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and protects the stomach lining. The active flavonoids and glycosides in licorice root reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, nausea, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. By promoting the healing of the stomach lining and reducing inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules can help alleviate gastritis symptoms and promote a faster recovery, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues. Regular use can also help prevent future gastritis episodes.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with gastritis by reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membrane lining of the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Glycyrrhizin: Contribute to the treatment of gastritis by reducing inflammation, protecting the stomach lining, and inhibiting the production of stomach acid, which helps to soothe and heal the stomach.

Flavonoids: Aid in the management of gastritis by exhibiting antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation, and protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, thereby promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Barks: The barks are used to make capsules as they contain similar compounds to the roots, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Leaves: The leaves are sometimes used to make capsules as they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the stomach and reduce symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly in a mesh strainer under cold running water for 3 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots further in a food dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift the powdered roots through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to the pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. Additionally, ginger has a natural antacid effect, helping to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate heartburn and nausea. By promoting digestion and reducing inflammation, Zingiber officinale capsules provide relief from gastritis symptoms, promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach, which are common symptoms of gastritis.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis.
Catechins: Catechins have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress in the stomach, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for gastritis due to their high concentration of active compounds that help soothe and calm the stomach lining.
Roots: The roots contain a high amount of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach.
Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale contain a combination of active compounds that help in reducing nausea and inflammation, making them a useful component in capsules for gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 zingiber officinale roots with a length of 1-2 inches and dry them in a shaded area for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered root with 2 parts of a filler material such as cellulose or starch.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 mg of the powdered root mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. The bioactive compound curcumin present in these capsules has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties that protect the stomach lining from oxidative damage, promoting healing and preventing further inflammation, making Curcuma longa capsules a natural and effective remedy for gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with gastritis.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent damage to the stomach lining, thereby providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a smaller amount of curcumin, but also have other beneficial compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.

Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Curcuma longa are also a source of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that contribute to the anti-inflammatory and healing effects of the capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried curcuma longa roots and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground curcuma longa powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 1/2 cup of distilled water to the bowl and heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before filling gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with gastritis because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and soothes the stomach lining. The plant's alkaloids, particularly quinidine and quinine, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as abdominal pain, bloating, and nausea. By regulating digestive enzymes and promoting a healthy gut environment, Cinchona officinalis capsules may help to calm inflammation and promote healing in the stomach, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Quinic acid: This compound has been shown to have protective effects on the gastrointestinal tract, helping to prevent damage and promote healing in gastritis.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent bleeding, and promote the formation of a protective barrier in the stomach lining to help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain quinine and other alkaloids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach lining and relieve gastritis symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis contain flavonoids and alkaloids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in late autumn or early spring when the bark is at its highest alkaloid content.
Dry the cinchona officinalis bark in a warm place at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 weeks.
Grind 30 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered bark with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose powder and 10 grams of tapioca starch.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture and seal using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with gastritis because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The gel of the Aloe vera plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastritis. It helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Aloe barbadensis capsules provide relief from gastritis and support overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: They help soothe and protect the mucous lining in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
Polysaccharides: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as bloating and abdominal pain.

Anthraquinones (specifically Aloe-emodin): They have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help relax the digestive tract muscles and reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and soothing properties.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from the leaves contains mucilages that help protect and soothe the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of aloe barbadensis gel from 4-6 mature plants grown in full sun and harvested in summer.
Dry the gel in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 12 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried aloe barbadensis gel into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 30 seconds.
Mix 50g of the powdered aloe barbadensis with 20g of vegetable cellulose powder as a capsule filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe barbadensis mixture using a capsule filler machine within 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining. The peppermint oil in the capsules relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for easier digestion and reducing symptoms such as bloating and cramps. Additionally, peppermint oil has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing nausea and heartburn, which are common symptoms of gastritis. This natural remedy provides relief from gastritis symptoms, promoting a healthier digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with gastritis by reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: Relieves gastritis symptoms by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
Menthol: Eases gastritis by reducing inflammation and providing relief from nausea and vomiting due to its cooling and analgesic effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, particularly menthol and menthone, help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and symptoms of gastritis.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita contain a higher concentration of essential oils, which can aid in digestion and reduce stomach inflammation when used in capsules.
Stems: The stems of the plant also contain essential oils and other compounds that may help to calm the stomach and reduce symptoms of gastritis when consumed in capsule form.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 30-40°c for 2 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to reduce the dried material to a fine powder.
Mix 1g of the powder with 1g of gelatin and 10ml of water in a small bowl.
Fill 20 gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with gastritis because they contain the active compound anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The capsules also contain other beneficial compounds like fenchone and limonene, which have natural anti-acid and anti-spasmodic effects. This helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain. The herbal remedy promotes digestion and healing, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Anethole: This phenolic compound has carminative properties, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and relieve symptoms of bloating and cramps associated with gastritis.
Trans-anethole: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Seeds: Used because they contain a high concentration of essential oils, including anethole and fenchone, which possess anti-inflammatory and carminative properties to soothe the stomach.
Fruits: Used due to their rich content of essential oils, including anethole and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects to provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 500 grams of foeniculum vulgare roots and rhizomes to ensure sufficient quantity for capsules.
Dry the foeniculum vulgare roots and rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 50 grams of gelatin to create a uniform capsule blend.
Encapsulate the foeniculum vulgare-gelatin mixture in size 00 capsules using a manual encapsulation machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with gastritis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The herb also contains antioxidants that protect the stomach from oxidative stress, promoting healing and preventing further damage. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been shown to enhance the immune system, which can aid in the recovery process and prevent recurring gastritis episodes, ultimately leading to a faster and more effective recovery. This makes it a popular natural remedy for gastritis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Saponins: Saponins have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the stomach lining from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating gastritis.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the stomach, thus providing relief from gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary source of astragaloside IV and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the gastric mucosa.
Roots: Roots also contain polysaccharides that have immunomodulatory and gastroprotective effects, helping to soothe and heal gastritis.

Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus have been traditionally used to enhance the body's defense mechanisms and promote healing in gastritis, which is often attributed to their rich content of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of dried astragalus membranaceus roots and stems from a reputable source.
Clean and dry the roots and stems in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 1-2 cups of the dried roots and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered roots and stems with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of water to create a liquid solution.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500-1000 mg of the liquid solution and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with gastritis because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginseng help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gastritis. Additionally, ginseng has antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing the risk of gastritis flare-ups. This can lead to improved digestion and reduced symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: They help with gastritis by reducing inflammation and improving the integrity of the gastrointestinal mucosa.
Saponins: They help with gastritis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage.
Phenolic acids: They help with gastritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and reducing oxidative stress in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary source of ginsenosides, the active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes also contain high concentrations of ginsenosides, making them a valuable part for gastritis treatment.
Leaves: Although lesser than roots and rhyzomes, leaves also contain some ginsenosides and are used to support the overall health benefits of Panax ginseng.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 2 grams of dried panax ginseng root and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix 1 gram of the ginseng powder with 5 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 10 milliliters of glycerin and 20 milliliters of distilled water to the gelatin mixture.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes until the gelatin is fully dissolved and forms a gel.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill gelatin capsules with the ginseng gel mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as pain and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba may improve blood flow to the stomach, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. This may help to calm digestive issues and promote a sense of well-being, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, which can contribute to gastritis symptoms.
Terpenoids (specifically Ginkgolides A and B): Inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical that can stimulate stomach acid production and exacerbate gastritis.
Bilobalide: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used in capsules for gastritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used in capsules for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in capsules for gastritis as they contain compounds that help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, promoting healing and comfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and measure out 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in a clean environment.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems into a fine powder using a high-speed blender.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 400 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba powder using a capsule filler.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dark place for up to one year.
